claim: Galgos are behaviorally more similar to greyhounds (laid back, lazy) while podencos are more similar to lurchers (energetic, active, playful) — the two breeds have distinctly different behavioral profiles despite sharing similar trauma and abandonment contexts.
stance_target: galgo and podenco require different behavioral priors in the lost dog model — galgo defaults to lower arousal/higher freeze, podenco to higher arousal/more active movement
scope_conditions: Spanish sighthounds; rescue and adoption context; Kent Greyhound Rescue field experience
quoted_support: In general galgos tend to be more greyhound like (laid back and lazy) and podencos more lurcher like (full of fun for the more active family).

claim: Both galgos and podencos can cohabit with cats when introduced correctly, but behavioral assessment is difficult for dogs coming directly from a pound or hunter — meaning behavioral profiling at rescue intake is inherently uncertain.
stance_target: galgo/podenco behavioral data from hunting/rescue backgrounds has high uncertainty at intake; probabilistic priors must carry wide confidence intervals
scope_conditions: galgo and podenco from non-home environments (shelter, hunter direct)
quoted_support: It is really difficult to assess dogs to live with young children when they have come from a non home environment, for example the dog pound or direct from a hunter.
